alerts.title: US official confirms US-Iran 'agreement in principle' to reopen Strait of Hormuz
alerts.summary: The New York Times, citing a US official, reports Washington and Tehran have agreed in principle to a deal reopening the Strait of Hormuz, corroborated by BBC and PBS quoting Trump that the framework is 'largely negotiated.' DW.com describes both sides striking an 'upbeat tone,' though Trump simultaneously told negotiators 'not to rush' (BBC, Politico), signaling the text is not yet locked.

alerts.title: Khamenei: enriched uranium must stay in Iran, clashing with Trump pledge to Israel
alerts.summary: Reuters exclusive cites Iranian sources saying the Supreme Leader insists enriched uranium remain on Iranian soil, while ynetnews reports Trump assured Israel there would be no deal without dismantling the nuclear program. The Jerusalem Post and Haaretz flag contradictory US-Iran claims on the nuclear track as the central sticking point with breakout time still assessed at 1-2 weeks at 60% U-235.

alerts.title: Houthi missile strike on Israel reignites fears of Red Sea shipping campaign
alerts.summary: AP and The Times of Israel report a Houthi ballistic missile attack on Israel has revived concerns of renewed Ansar Allah strikes on Red Sea commercial shipping, with BBC and Global Security Review warning of potential global economic damage. Proxy status board lists Houthis as 'Elevated - Multiple Recent Attacks' while USS Eisenhower, Bataan, Carney, Mason and Laboon remain concentrated off the Yemeni coast.

alerts.title: US deploys defensive 'umbrella' over Hormuz shipping as Trump weighs fresh Iran strikes
alerts.summary: DefenseScoop reports the US is deploying an 'umbrella' of defense and tech assets to shield commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz, while The New York Times says Trump is simultaneously weighing options for new strikes inside Iran even as the diplomatic track advances. USS Florida (SSGN) remains on station in the Persian Gulf and ynetnews characterizes the buildup as testing the regional balance of power.

alerts.title: NATO 'Arcade Strike' exercise rehearses deep strikes on Russia from London hub
alerts.summary: DeepState UA (~196K views) reports NATO's British-led Allied Rapid Reaction Corps (ARRC) stood up a headquarters on the disused Charing Cross Underground platform to wargame deep strikes into Russia in response to a simulated attack on a Baltic ally, testing EW, comms-jamming and counter-drone capabilities. The exercise marks an unusually public NATO signal of contingency planning amid ongoing Zircon/Oreshnik strikes on Kyiv.

analysis_note: Washington and Tehran appear hours from a Hormuz framework deal even as the nuclear-dismantlement question remains unresolved, Trump keeps a strike option open, and Houthi missiles plus a US defensive 'umbrella' over Gulf shipping show the military track running in parallel to diplomacy.
